input: Last quarter we lost our three biggest accounts in the same week. All three said the same thing on the way out: "You stopped feeling like the small team that used to answer in an hour." I'd spent eighteen months hiring to scale support and the exact thing customers loved had quietly died in the process. This is the post I wish someone had handed me before I broke it — what actually changes when a support team crosses fifteen people, which of your early habits stop working, and the three we rebuilt from scratch to keep response times under an hour without burning anyone out. None of it is theory. All of it cost us money to learn.

A landing page has one job: turn a stranger into a lead. Most of the ones I audit fail at it in the first five seconds, and almost always for the same boring reason — the visitor can't tell what the thing is. Over the last year I've torn down 200 SaaS landing pages for clients and kept score. Below are the seven patterns that show up on nearly every page that converts under 1%, ranked by how much lift I've seen from fixing each one. Number three cost one client an estimated $40k a month and took eleven minutes to fix. Start there if you only read one section.

In today's fast-paced digital landscape, businesses of all sizes are constantly looking for new and innovative ways to stand out from the competition. With so many channels and strategies available, it can be difficult to know where to begin. That's where content marketing comes in. Content marketing has become one of the most powerful tools in the modern marketer's toolkit, and understanding how to leverage it effectively is more important than ever. In this comprehensive guide, we'll explore everything you need to know about content marketing and how it can help take your business to the next level.

Customer churn is defined as the rate at which customers stop doing business with a company over a given period of time. It is typically expressed as a percentage and is a key metric for any subscription-based business. Understanding churn is essential for long-term growth. In this article, we will discuss what churn is, why it matters, and some strategies you can use to reduce it. By the end, you'll have a solid understanding of this important concept and be better equipped to keep your customers happy and engaged for the long haul.

Here are 10 email marketing tips that will help you grow your list and increase your open rates. Email marketing remains one of the most effective channels for reaching your audience, and with the right approach, you can see real results. Whether you're just getting started or looking to improve your existing strategy, these tips have something for everyone. Let's dive in and take a look at how you can make the most of your email marketing efforts this year and beyond. There's a lot to cover, so grab a coffee and let's get started on this journey together.

I switched our entire ad budget from Facebook to TikTok on a Monday. By Friday our cost per acquisition had dropped 38% and I nearly got fired for not asking first. Here's the uncomfortable part nobody tells you: it wasn't the platform. We ran the same creative on both and TikTok won because the format forced us to stop making ads that looked like ads. This post breaks down the exact three creative rules that transferred — and the two that blew up in our faces when we tried to scale past $10k a day. If you're staring at a rising CAC and wondering whether to jump platforms, read this before you move a dollar.

When it comes to choosing the best CRM software for your small business, there are many factors to consider. The best CRM software can make a huge difference in how you manage your customer relationships. In this guide to the best CRM software, we'll look at the top options and help you find the best CRM software for your needs. Choosing the right CRM software is one of the most important decisions a small business can make, so it's worth taking the time to get it right. Let's explore the best CRM software options available today.

Everyone tells founders to "talk to your customers." Almost nobody tells you that most customer interviews are worthless, and that the worthless ones feel the most productive. For two years I ran interviews that left me energized and taught me nothing, because I was asking people to predict their own future behavior — the one thing humans are reliably terrible at. Then a former Intercom researcher spent an afternoon rewriting my questions, and the signal I got the next week was so different it reshaped the roadmap. Here's the shift: stop asking what people want, start excavating what they've already done. Below is the exact interview script, and the four question types I've banned forever.

Building a brand is one of the most important things a company can do. A strong brand helps you stand out, build trust, and create lasting relationships with your customers. But building a brand isn't easy, and there are many things to consider along the way. From your visual identity to your tone of voice, every detail matters. In this post, we'll walk through the key elements of building a brand that resonates with your audience and stands the test of time. Whether you're a startup or an established business, these principles will help guide you.

Our onboarding email sequence had a 4% conversion to paid. Industry benchmark is around 15%. For six months I A/B tested subject lines, send times, and button colors like a good little growth marketer, and moved the number exactly nowhere. The problem, it turned out, had nothing to do with the emails. It was that we activated users on day one around a feature they didn't care about until day nine. Once we reordered the sequence to match when value actually landed, conversion tripled in a month with zero copy changes. This is a post about why your email tests are probably measuring the wrong thing — and how to find the moment that actually matters.

SEO can seem overwhelming, especially if you're just getting started. There's a lot of information out there, and it can be hard to know what's actually true and what's just noise. The good news is that the fundamentals of SEO haven't changed all that much over the years. In this beginner's guide, we'll break down the basics of SEO in a way that's easy to understand, so you can start improving your rankings and driving more organic traffic to your website. By the end, you'll have a clear roadmap for where to focus your efforts.

Skincare doesn't have to be complicated. In fact, we think the best routines are the simplest ones. That's why we created a line of products designed to give your skin exactly what it needs — nothing more, nothing less. Made with clean, thoughtfully sourced ingredients and free from the stuff you don't want, our products are designed to work with your skin, not against it. In this post, we'll share our philosophy on simple skincare and walk you through how to build a routine that actually fits into your busy life. Because good skin shouldn't be a full-time job.

For eight years I've been helping brands grow on Instagram, and if there's one thing I've learned, it's that there's no magic bullet. Growth takes time, patience, and a willingness to experiment. Over the years I've seen accounts blow up overnight and others grow slowly and steadily, and honestly, the steady ones tend to last longer. In this post I want to share some of the hard-won lessons I've picked up along the way, from the mistakes I made early on to the mindset shifts that made all the difference. If you've been feeling stuck, I hope this gives you a fresh perspective on your own growth journey.

Are you ready to take your business to the next level? If you're like most entrepreneurs, you're always looking for ways to grow faster and work smarter. In this post, we're going to share some game-changing strategies that can help you unlock your full potential and achieve the success you've always dreamed of. These are the same strategies used by some of the most successful companies in the world, and now you can use them too. So if you're ready to transform your business and start seeing real results, keep reading.

The best productivity system is the one you'll actually use. It sounds obvious, but so many of us fall into the trap of chasing the newest app or method, convinced that this time it'll finally click. The truth is, productivity isn't about tools — it's about habits, mindset, and consistency. In this post, we'll explore what really makes a productivity system work, why most people abandon theirs within weeks, and how you can finally build one that sticks. By the end, you'll have a clearer sense of what actually matters and be ready to stop app-hopping for good.

Remote work is here to stay. Over the past few years, we've seen a massive shift in how and where people work, and there's no going back. But managing a remote team comes with its own unique set of challenges. From communication to collaboration to company culture, leaders have had to rethink the way they operate. In this article, we'll explore some best practices for managing remote teams effectively, so you can keep your team happy, productive, and connected no matter where they're working from. Let's take a closer look at what it takes to lead in a remote-first world.

It's important to note that navigating the complexities of modern B2B sales requires a strategic approach. In today's competitive environment, sales teams must leverage a variety of tools and techniques to stay ahead. Understanding the nuances of the buyer's journey is crucial for driving conversions and building lasting relationships. In this article, we will delve into the key strategies that can help your sales team succeed, exploring proven methods and actionable insights that you can implement right away to unlock the full potential of your sales process.

The average B2B sales cycle for deals over $50k is 84 days. Ours was 41, and the difference came down to a single email we sent after the first call — before the prospect had even asked for a proposal. Most reps wait to be invited deeper into the process. We stopped waiting. This post is the exact framework our team uses to compress a three-month cycle in half without being pushy, including the after-call email template that does most of the work and the one qualifying question we ask on every first call that kills bad deals early. If your pipeline is full but nothing's closing, the problem is probably timing, not product.

Pricing is hard. There's no way around it. Setting the right price for your product or service can feel like a guessing game, and getting it wrong can have serious consequences for your business. But it doesn't have to be so difficult. With the right approach, you can develop a pricing strategy that works for you and your customers. In this post, we'll cover some of the most common pricing strategies and help you figure out which one is right for your business. By the end, you'll feel much more confident about how to price your offering.

Three years ago I raised our prices 40% overnight and sent an email I was sure would tank the business. Instead, we lost 6% of customers and grew revenue 31% in a quarter. The part that still bugs me: the customers who left were the ones who'd generated 80% of our support tickets. I'd been terrified of a decision that turned out to be the healthiest thing we ever did. This post is the actual email I sent, the math I ran to talk myself into it, and the three signals that told me our price was too low long before I had the nerve to act. If raising prices makes you queasy, that queasiness is probably the tell.

Welcome to our blog! We're so excited to have you here. In this space, we'll be sharing all kinds of helpful tips, insights, and stories about marketing, growth, and building a business you love. Our goal is to create a community of like-minded people who are passionate about doing great work and making an impact. Whether you're a seasoned pro or just starting out, we hope you'll find something valuable here. So grab a cup of coffee, get comfortable, and let's start this journey together. We can't wait to see where it takes us.

Cold email is dead, or so the LinkedIn gurus keep announcing every eighteen months. Meanwhile, cold email booked 60% of our meetings last quarter. What actually died is the spray-and-pray version — the 500-a-day blast with a merge tag and a prayer. The version that works now looks almost nothing like it, and requires research most people won't do. In this post I'll show you the exact three-line structure that gets us a 22% reply rate, why personalization tokens are a trap, and the single line of research that separates an email that gets a reply from one that gets marked as spam. Fair warning: it's more work than the gurus admit.

A/B testing is a powerful way to improve your website and increase conversions. By testing different versions of a page, you can learn what resonates with your audience and make data-driven decisions. But A/B testing can be tricky if you don't know what you're doing. There are a lot of pitfalls to avoid, and it's easy to draw the wrong conclusions from your data. In this post, we'll cover the basics of A/B testing and share some tips to help you run tests that actually move the needle for your business. Let's get started.

Most "how we grew to $1M ARR" posts are survivorship bias with a nice chart. I read forty of them before writing our own, and every single one made the path look inevitable. Ours wasn't. We nearly shut down at month fourteen, pivoted away from the feature that's now our entire business, and grew for a full year on a channel I was convinced would never work. This is the honest version, including the two months of flat revenue I usually edit out of the deck, the specific decision that saved us, and the growth channel every advisor told me to abandon. If you want the inspiring version, there are forty others. This one has the ugly parts left in.

Great design is all about the details. It's the little things that separate a good product from a truly great one. When it comes to designing digital products, there are countless decisions to make, from color and typography to layout and interaction. Each of these choices plays a role in the overall experience. In this post, we'll take a look at some principles of good design and how you can apply them to your own work. Whether you're a designer or not, understanding these fundamentals will help you create better products and delight your users.

We spent $12,000 on a rebrand and our conversion rate didn't move a single point. Here's what did move it: changing one word in our hero headline, which I did on a Tuesday afternoon for free. I'm not saying branding doesn't matter — I'm saying we spent money on the visible thing because it felt like progress, while ignoring the invisible thing that actually drives the number. This post is about where marketing budgets go to die: the expensive, satisfying projects that photograph well and change nothing. I'll show you the four we wasted money on, the cheap changes that beat them, and how to tell the difference before you sign the invoice.

Social media is an essential part of any modern marketing strategy. With billions of people using platforms like Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn every day, there's a huge opportunity to reach your target audience where they already spend their time. But with so many platforms and so much competition, standing out can be a real challenge. In this guide, we'll share some proven strategies for growing your presence on social media and building a loyal following. No matter what platform you're focused on, these tips will help you get more out of your efforts.

There's a specific kind of quiet that settles over a Slack channel when a launch flops. We had it for about six hours after our Product Hunt launch — the one we'd spent three weeks preparing — finished the day at #14 with 89 upvotes. I want to walk you through exactly what we did, what we spent, and why it didn't work, because the post-mortem taught me more than any success ever did. Specifically, I'll cover the three assumptions we made about our audience that were flat wrong, the timing mistake I still can't believe I made, and what I'd do differently with the same product tomorrow.

Your website is often the first impression people have of your business, so it's important to make it count. A well-designed website can help you build credibility, attract new customers, and grow your business. But what makes a website truly effective? In this post, we'll explore the key elements of a high-performing website and share some tips for creating a site that not only looks great but also drives results. Whether you're building a new site or refreshing an existing one, these insights will point you in the right direction.

Last Tuesday I sat down with a coffee and read all 47 case studies on our biggest competitor's website. It took me the better part of a morning, and my desk was covered in sticky notes by the end. Some of the case studies were long, some were short, and a few didn't really say much of anything. I made a spreadsheet with columns for industry, company size, and the quote they pulled. It was a fascinating exercise and I learned a lot about how they present themselves to the world. Competitive research is something every marketer should do more of, honestly.

Understanding your target audience is the foundation of any successful marketing campaign. Before you can create content, run ads, or launch a product, you need to know who you're trying to reach. This means going beyond basic demographics and really getting to know your ideal customer — their hopes, their fears, their pain points, and their goals. In this article, we'll explore how to build a detailed customer persona that will guide all of your marketing decisions and help you connect with the people who matter most to your business.

Newsletters are having a moment, and for good reason. In a world of algorithms and shrinking organic reach, owning a direct line to your audience has never been more valuable. A newsletter lets you show up in someone's inbox on your terms, build a real relationship over time, and drive results you actually control. If you've been thinking about starting a newsletter but aren't sure where to begin, you're in the right place. In this guide, we'll cover everything from choosing a topic to growing your list to keeping readers engaged over the long term.

The retention curve tells you everything, and almost nobody looks at it correctly. Most teams stare at the top-line churn number, panic, and start plugging leaks at random. What they miss is the shape of the curve — specifically, whether it flattens. A curve that flattens means you've found a group of people for whom the product is genuinely sticky; a curve that keeps dropping to zero means you haven't, and no amount of win-back emails will save you. This post shows you how to read your own retention curve in about ten minutes, what a flattening curve looks like versus a doomed one, and the single cohort you should obsess over.

Video content is more popular than ever. From YouTube to TikTok to Instagram Reels, people are consuming more video than at any point in history. For brands, this represents a massive opportunity to connect with audiences in a more engaging and authentic way. But creating video content can feel intimidating, especially if you don't have a big budget or a professional production team. The good news is that you don't need either to get started. In this post, we'll share some practical tips for creating great video content on a budget.
